Docs / Core Concepts / Authentication

Authentication

DevRef includes built-in authentication with support for OAuth providers, API keys, and session-based auth. No external dependencies required.

OAuth Providers

Connect any OAuth 2.0 provider with a few lines of configuration. GitHub, Google, and GitLab are supported out of the box.

import { createAuth } from 'devref/auth'
const auth = createAuth({
  providers: [
    'github',
    'google',
  ],
  secret: process.env.AUTH_SECRET,
})

API Keys

For server-to-server communication, use API key authentication. Keys are scoped to specific permissions and can be rotated without downtime.

const key = await auth.createApiKey({
  name: 'CI Pipeline',
  scopes: ['read:docs', 'write:deploy'],
  expiresIn: '90d',
})

Session Management

Sessions are stored server-side by default with automatic cookie handling. You can switch to JWT-based sessions for stateless deployments.

Tip
Use the useSession() hook in any page component to access the current user and auth state.
{{footer}}
